scenario: Vertex Insurer routes 9000 events per day into Camunda 8.8 message start events that spin up new policy applications. A second message with the same correlationKey arrives while the first application instance is still active, and the integration team needs the documented behavior.
question: How does a published message with a correlationKey affect instance creation at a message start event when a matching instance is already active?

options.text: If a process instance is already active and was started by a message with the same correlationKey, the new message is dropped silently and never buffered for retry
If a process instance is already active and was started by a message with the same correlationKey, the new message is not correlated and is buffered when its time to live is greater than 0
If a process instance is already active and was started by a message with the same correlationKey, the new message always spawns an additional concurrent instance of the same process version
If a process instance is already active and was started by a message with the same correlationKey, the new message is correlated to that running instance as if it were a catch event

a.text: Incorrect. The documentation specifies buffering rather than dropping; if the message has a time to live greater than 0 it is held until the active instance completes or is terminated.

b.text: Correct. The documentation states that if an instance with the same correlationKey is active, the message is not correlated and a new instance is not created; the message is buffered when its time to live is greater than 0.

c.text: Incorrect. A duplicate correlationKey suppresses new instance creation in the documented behavior; the engine does not spawn additional concurrent instances of the same correlated value.

d.text: Incorrect. The documentation says the message is not correlated to the active instance in this case; instead it is buffered for a later instance once the active one finishes.

explanation: Message start events do not create a new instance when an active instance with the same correlationKey exists; the message is buffered as long as its time to live is greater than 0 and consumed when the active instance ends.
